title: Data Dictionary for NYC_311_SR_2010-2020-sample-1M.csv
description:
**Description**

This dataset contains one million 311 complaint records from New York City, spanning the period 1 January 2010 to 23 December 2020. Each record is uniquely identified by an integer key and includes a timestamp of when the complaint was created, the agency responsible for handling it (e.g., NYPD, HPD), the type and descriptor of the problem, spatial information (address components, latitude/longitude, state‑plane coordinates), as well as administrative fields such as status, due date, resolution narrative, and community‑board jurisdiction. The data were aggregated from multiple city agencies and reflect a wide range of complaint categories—from noise and heating to parking violations—although more than half of the records are labeled “Other” in both type and descriptor, indicating incomplete or generic entries.

**Notable Characteristics**

- **Central tendency & spread** – Created dates cluster around early‑to‑mid 2010s with a long tail into late 2020; most complaints were closed within a few months (median time between created and closed dates ≈ 6 months).  
- **Distribution shape** – Complaint type, descriptor, and location‑type fields are highly skewed: the top five categories account for only ~20 % of records while “Other” comprises >50 %. The same holds for agency distribution (NYPD and HPD together cover ~52 %).  
- **Missing values & data quality** – Large proportions of missing or null entries in address‑related fields: 239 k NULL location types, 55 k NULL incident ZIPs, 174 k NULL incident addresses, and 254 k NULL latitude/longitude. These gaps limit geospatial analysis.  
- **Outliers & anomalies** – Date columns contain sentinel extremes (1900‑01‑01 for closed dates, 2100‑01‑01 for future placeholders) that likely represent “not yet resolved” or “unknown” cases.  
- **Duplicates** – The `Unique Key` field is truly unique across the dataset; no duplicate complaint records are present.  
- **PII/PHI considerations** – While personal identifiers (names, phone numbers) have been removed, detailed address components and exact latitude/longitude coordinates can still be used to locate individuals or small groups, so care should be taken when sharing or publishing subsets of the data.  
- **Spatial coverage** – The majority of records fall within the five NYC boroughs; 8 % reference parks, and a small subset involves bridge/highway incidents. State‑plane coordinates are available for only ~70 % of records.  
- **Temporal gaps** – Certain agency activity periods show abrupt changes (e.g., a spike in HPD complaints around 2014), which may reflect policy shifts or reporting changes rather than actual incident frequency.

Unique Key.title: Record ID
Unique Key.description: A system‑generated integer that uniquely identifies each complaint record.

Created Date.title: Created Date
Created Date.description: The date and time the complaint was originally submitted to the city’s open data portal, in mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss AM/PM format.

Closed Date.title: Closed Date
Closed Date.description: The date and time when the complaint was officially closed or marked as resolved; may be null for unresolved cases, in mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss AM/PM format.

Agency.title: Agency Code
Agency.description: Short code used to identify the city agency responsible for handling the complaint (e.g., NYPD, HPD).

Agency Name.title: Agency Full Name
Agency Name.description: Full official name of the agency that responded to or processed the complaint.

Incident Zip.title: Incident ZIP Code
Incident Zip.description: Five‑digit ZIP code corresponding to the address or area where the complaint was reported.

Incident Address.title: Incident Street Address
Incident Address.description: Complete street address where the incident occurred, including building number and any additional qualifiers. Components include Street Name, City, and Incident Zip.

Street Name.title: Primary Street Name
Street Name.description: Main street on which the incident took place; part of the full incident address.

Cross Street 1.title: Cross Street 1
Cross Street 1.description: First cross street that intersects or is nearest to the incident location; may be null if not applicable.

Cross Street 2.title: Cross Street 2
Cross Street 2.description: Second cross street, if applicable, that intersects or is nearest to the incident location; may be null.

Intersection Street 1.title: Intersection Street 1
Intersection Street 1.description: One of the streets forming an intersection at the incident site; may be null.

Intersection Street 2.title: Intersection Street 2
Intersection Street 2.description: Second street forming an intersection at the incident site; may be null.

Address Type.title: Address Type
Address Type.description: Indicates whether the reported address is a specific building, block face, intersection, or latitude/longitude coordinate. This classification helps interpret other address components.

City.title: City / Borough
City.description: City in which the incident was reported; for NYC data this is typically one of the borough names. Together with Incident ZIP and Street Name it forms part of the mailing address.

Landmark.title: Nearby Landmark
Landmark.description: Notable nearby landmark or point of reference at the incident location, used to aid locating the site when precise street address is unavailable.

Facility Type.title: Facility Type
Facility Type.description: Type of facility involved in the complaint (e.g., school district, DSNY garage).

Status.title: Complaint Status
Status.description: Current status of the complaint (Closed, Pending, Open, etc.).

Due Date.title: Due Date
Due Date.description: Scheduled date and time by which a response or resolution is expected; may be null for open cases.

Resolution Description.title: Resolution Narrative
Resolution Description.description: Narrative detailing how the complaint was resolved, including actions taken or outcomes achieved.

Resolution Action Updated Date.title: Last Resolution Update
Resolution Action Updated Date.description: Date of most recent update to the resolution status or action; indicates latest activity on the case.

Community Board.title: Community Board
Community Board.description: Community board number and borough designation with jurisdiction over the incident location (e.g., 12 MANHATTAN).

BBL.title: BBL Identifier
BBL.description: Borough‑Block‑Lot identifier, a numeric key used by NYC for parcel and property data.

Borough.title: Incident Borough
Borough.description: NYC borough in which the incident occurred; aligns with City field but is more specific when City is null.

X Coordinate (State Plane).title: X Coordinate (State Plane)
X Coordinate (State Plane).description: East‑west coordinate value in the New York State Plane projection system, expressed as an integer.

Y Coordinate (State Plane).title: Y Coordinate (State Plane)
Y Coordinate (State Plane).description: North‑south coordinate value in the New York State Plane projection system, expressed as an integer.

Open Data Channel Type.title: Open Data Channel
Open Data Channel Type.description: Method by which the complaint was submitted to the open data portal (PHONE, ONLINE, etc.).

Park Facility Name.title: Park Facility Name
Park Facility Name.description: Name of a park or recreational facility associated with the incident, if applicable.

Park Borough.title: Park Borough
Park Borough.description: Borough where the referenced park facility is located; may differ from Incident Borough when the complaint references a park in another borough.

Vehicle Type.title: Vehicle Type
Vehicle Type.description: Type of vehicle involved in the incident (e.g., Car Service, Green Taxi).

Taxi Company Borough.title: Taxi Company Borough
Taxi Company Borough.description: Borough where a taxi company operates or was identified at the time of the incident; often used for taxi‑related complaints.

Taxi Pick Up Location.title: Taxi Pick‑Up Location
Taxi Pick Up Location.description: Location where a taxi was picked up, often an airport or intersection; may be null if not applicable.

Bridge Highway Name.title: Bridge/Highway Name
Bridge Highway Name.description: Name of the bridge or highway associated with the incident location.

Road Ramp.title: Road Ramp Indicator
Road Ramp.description: Indicates whether a road ramp was involved in the incident, with possible values such as Roadway or N/A.

Bridge Highway Segment.title: Bridge/Highway Segment
Bridge Highway Segment.description: Specific segment of a bridge or highway (e.g., Ramp, Roadway) where the incident occurred; complements Bridge Highway Name and Direction.

Latitude.title: Latitude
Latitude.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the incident location in decimal degrees. Together with Longitude it forms a geographic coordinate pair.

Longitude.title: Longitude
Longitude.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the incident location in decimal degrees. Together with Latitude it forms a geographic coordinate pair.

Location.title: Location (Lat/Long)
Location.description: String representation of latitude and longitude coordinates enclosed in parentheses, e.g., "(40.1123853, -77.5195844)"; derived from Latitude and Longitude fields.
